concept: Vaccination Center
fhir_definition: Name of the location as used by humans. Does not need to be unique.
search_parameters: A portion of the location's name or alias

concept: Medical region of vaccination center
fhir_definition: hysical form of the location, e.g. building, room, vehicle, road, virtual.
search_parameters: A server defined search that may match any of the string fields in the Address, including line, city, district, state, country, postalCode, and/or text. Can be string or token based.

concept: Date of adverse reaction occurence
fhir_definition: The date on which the existence of the AdverseEvent was first recorded
search_parameters: Occurence, date. When the suspect entity occurred
Notes: Another resource that can be mapped onto is Observation
Another option is to map to Immunization resource under element Immunization.reaction.date

concept: Month of adverse reaction occurence
fhir_definition: The month on which the existence of the AdverseEvent was first recorded
search_parameters: Occurence, date. When the suspect entity occurred
Notes: Another resource that can be mapped onto is Observation
Another option is to map to Immunization resource under element Immunization.reaction.date

concept: Patient identifier
fhir_definition: An identifier for this patient
search_parameters: Patients are almost always assigned specific numerical identifiers. Search identifier as token.

concept: Patient name
fhir_definition: A name associated with the individual.
search_parameters: A server defined search that may match any of the string fields in the HumanName, including family, given, prefix, suffix, and/or text

concept: Patient gender
fhir_definition: Administrative Gender - the gender that the patient is considered to have for administration and record keeping purposes.

concept: Patient date of birth
fhir_definition: The date of birth for the individual.

concept: Patient residential address
fhir_definition: An address for the individual.
search_parameters: A server defined search that may match any of the string fields in the Address, including line, city, district, state, country, postalCode, and/or text.

concept: Patient weight
fhir_definition: Body weight of the individual

concept: Type of vaccine administered to patient
fhir_definition: Vaccine that was administered or was to be administered.
terminology: HL7 Value Set http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/vaccine-code. SNOMED CT. CVX.
Notes: Type of vaccines given to patient can also be mapped on to AdverseEvent resource under AdverseEvent.suspectEntity

concept: Type of vaccine administered to patient
fhir_definition: Vaccine that was administered or was to be administered.
terminology: HL7 Value Set http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/vaccine-code. SNOMED CT. CVX.

concept: Type of vaccine administered to patient
fhir_definition: Vaccine that was administered or was to be administered.
terminology: HL7 Value Set http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/vaccine-code. SNOMED CT. CVX.

concept: Manufacturer of vaccine
fhir_definition: Name of vaccine manufacturer.

concept: Antigen ingredient of vaccine administered
fhir_definition: An indication of which product was administered to the patient. This is typically a more detailed representation of the concept conveyed by the vaccineCode data element. If a Medication resource is referenced, it may be to a stand-alone resource or a contained resource within the Immunization resource.
terminology: HL7 Value Set http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/vaccine-code. SNOMED CT. CVX.
Notes: Another resource more detailed could be medication resource with element: Medication.ingredient.item. OR immunization evaluation resource with element: ImmunizationEvaluation.targetDisease (more for research and clinical trials)

concept: Vaccine lot number
fhir_definition: Lot number of the vaccine product.

concept: Vaccine lot number
fhir_definition: Lot number of the vaccine product.

concept: Date of expiration for vaccine administered
fhir_definition: Date vaccine batch expires.

concept: Site of vaccine administertion
fhir_definition: Body site where vaccine was administered.

concept: Makering authorization number/import permit number is the regulatory approval issued by the national medicines authority, Drug Administration of Vietnam
fhir_definition: Business identifier for the authorization, typically assigned by the authorizing body.
search_parameters: Business identifier for the authorization, typically assigned by the authorizing body. Token.

fhir_definition: Immunization.occurrence[x]

concept: Date and time of when symptoms of adverese reaction started or occurred

concept: Time between reaction occurence and vaccine administration
fhir_resource: Does not map directly onto a FHIR resource or element
Notes: Can be mapped onto resource Condition if others also linked to this resource. Condition.onset[x] element with condition period or range.

fhir_resource: Does not map directly onto a FHIR resource or element
Notes: Suggested element that it could be mapped onto: AdverseEvent.outcome (Describes the type of outcome from the adverse event, such as resolved, recovering, ongoing, resolved-with-sequelae, or fatal.)

concept: Clinical symptom or condition of adverse reaction
fhir_definition: Information about the condition that occurred as a result of the adverse event, such as hives due to the exposure to a substance (for example, a drug or a chemical) or a broken leg as a result of the fall.
terminology: Coded to: Condition resource (Condition.code).  http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/condition-code. SNOMED CT. ICD11.MedRA
Notes: I have not provided the exact SNOMED CT codes/ICD11 codes because I think that is more at implementation level rather than at a higher level for analysis for purpose of this project

concept: Patient's allergy history
fhir_definition: The clinical status of the allergy or intolerance.

Parent header: History of illness/allergy to any allergen
concept: Allergy type
fhir_definition: Category of the identified substance.

concept: Type of medicine patient is allergic to
fhir_definition: Code for an allergy or intolerance statement (either a positive or a negated/excluded statement). This may be a code for a substance or pharmaceutical product that is considered to be responsible for the adverse reaction risk (e.g., "Latex"), an allergy or intolerance condition (e.g., "Latex allergy"), or a negated/excluded code for a specific substance or class (e.g., "No latex allergy") or a general or categorical negated statement (e.g., "No known allergy", "No known drug allergies"). Note: the substance for a specific reaction may be different from the substance identified as the cause of the risk, but it must be consistent with it. For instance, it may be a more specific substance (e.g. a brand medication) or a composite product that includes the identified substance. It must be clinically safe to only process the 'code' and ignore the 'reaction.substance'. If a receiving system is unable to confirm that AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance falls within the semantic scope of AllergyIntolerance.code, then the receiving system should ignore AllergyIntolerance.reaction.substance.
search_parameters: Code that identifies the allergy or intolerance. token

concept: Type of food patient is allergic to

Note: the substance for a specific reaction may be different from the substance identified as the cause of the risk, but it must be consistent with it. search_parameters: Code that identifies the allergy or intolerance. token

concept: Severity of allergy
fhir_definition: Clinical assessment of the severity of the reaction event as a whole, potentially considering multiple different manifestations.
search_parameters: mild | moderate | severe (of event as a whole). token

Parent header: Reaction -Managed at center / Reaction - Managed at home
concept: Severity of Adverse Reaction
fhir_definition: Assessment whether this event, or averted event, was of clinical importance.
search_parameters: Seriousness or gravity of the event. token

Parent header: Reaction - Managed at center / Reaction - Managed at home
concept: Was the event recorded
fhir_definition: Estimated or actual date the AdverseEvent began, in the opinion of the reporter.

concept: Management for adverse reaction
fhir_definition: A description of the scope and nature of the plan.

Parent header: DIAGNOSIS AND MANAGEMENT AT THE CENTER
concept: Detailed description of management done at the center
fhir_definition: A description of the scope and nature of the plan.

concept: Detailed description of management done at the center
fhir_definition: A description of the scope and nature of the plan.

concept: Detailed description of management done at the center
fhir_definition: A description of the scope and nature of the plan.

concept: Outpatient management of adverse reaction
fhir_definition: Concepts representing classification of patient encounter such as ambulatory (outpatient), inpatient, emergency, home health or others due to local variations.

concept: Was patient given adrenaline as part of management for adverse reaction
fhir_definition: Identifies the medication that was administered. This is either a link to a resource representing the details of the medication or a simple attribute carrying a code that identifies the medication from a known list of medications.
terminology: HL 7 Value Set / SNOMED CT coddes http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/medication-codes. For epineprhine (387362001)

concept: Other management provided for adverse reaction managed at the center
fhir_definition: The specific procedure that is performed. Use text if the exact nature of the procedure cannot be coded (e.g. "Laparoscopic Appendectomy").
terminology: HL 7 Value Set / SNOMED CT http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/procedure-code. Code for other.
Notes: For any other, can also be mapped to the "Notes" element of each resource

Parent header: DIAGNOSIS AND MANAGEMENT AT THE CENTER
concept: Was patient managed at home with monitoring
fhir_definition: Identifies what "kind" of plan this is to support differentiation between multiple co-existing plans; e.g. "Home health", "psychiatric", "asthma", "disease management", "wellness plan", etc.

Parent header: DIAGNOSIS AND MANAGEMENT AT THE CENTER
concept: Was the patient trasnferred to hospital
fhir_definition: Category or kind of location after discharge.
terminology: HL 7 Vaue Set http://hl7.org/fhir/ValueSet/encounter-discharge-disposition. Code for hospital transfer (other-hcf)

fhir_definition: Identifies what "kind" of plan this is to support differentiation between multiple co-existing plans; e.g. "Home health", "psychiatric", "asthma", "disease management", "wellness plan", etc.

concept: Did patient self admit to hospital
Outcome for patient with adverse reaction
fhir_definition: Describes the type of outcome from the adverse event, such as resolved, recovering, ongoing, resolved-with-sequelae, or fatal.

concept: Treatment start date
fhir_definition: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place). For many administrations, such as swallowing a tablet the use of dateTime is more appropriate.

concept: Treatment end date
fhir_definition: A specific date/time or interval of time during which the administration took place (or did not take place). For many administrations, such as swallowing a tablet the use of dateTime is more appropriate.

concept: Grade of Anaphylaxis
fhir_definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.

concept: Grade of Anaphylaxis
fhir_definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.

concept: Grade of Anaphylaxis
fhir_definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.

concept: Grade of Anaphylaxis
fhir_definition: A subjective assessment of the severity of the condition as evaluated by the clinician.

concept: Diagnosis
fhir_definition: The coded diagnosis or a reference to a Condition (with other resources referenced in the evidence.detail), the use property will indicate the purpose of this specific diagnosis.

concept: Date and time of resolution of symptoms
fhir_definition: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission. This is called "abatement" because of the many overloaded connotations associated with "remission" or "resolution" - Some conditions, such as chronic conditions, are never really resolved, but they can abate.

concept: Period of symptom occurence
fhir_definition: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission. This is called "abatement" because of the many overloaded connotations associated with "remission" or "resolution" - Some conditions, such as chronic conditions, are never really resolved, but they can abate.

concept: Enter date and time if phase 2 occurs
fhir_definition: The date or estimated date that the condition resolved or went into remission. This is called "abatement" because of the many overloaded connotations associated with "remission" or "resolution" - Some conditions, such as chronic conditions, are never really resolved, but they can abate.
Notes: Technically, this should be a category under condition as relapse or phase 2 and then anothr entry for date and time
